name: Half-a-Pound Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Line a 6-inch x 3-inch aluminum foil loaf pan with wax paper.
3. With an electric mixer, beat butter in a small bowl until soft and light.
4. Gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy.
5. Beat egg in another small bowl.
6. Gradually add the egg to the butter-sugar mixture, beating until light and fluffy.
7. Stir in vanilla extract.
8. Stir in flour to moisten; beat until light and smooth.
9. Spoon batter into prepared pan and distribute evenly.
10. Bake for about 55 minutes until edges separate from the pan.
11. The top should be golden and spring back when lightly touched in the center.
12. Cool in the pan for 15 minutes.
13. Loosen sides and remove by lifting ends of wax paper.
14. Cool on a wire rack.
15. Sprinkle with powdered sugar if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
Variations:
- NUT POUND CAKE: Stir 2 to 3 tablespoons finely minced walnuts or pecans into the batter.
- LEMON OR ORANGE POUND CAKE: Stir 1/2 teaspoon freshly grated lemon or orange peel into the batter.
- VIENNESE CHOC. TORTE: Slice cake into 4 horizontal layers. Spread a thin layer of apricot jam and a layer of chocolate frosting on the lower 3 layers. Stack layers. Frost sides and top with frosting. Sprinkle toasted almonds over the top.
